title: Shadows of Brimstone as a Solo RPG - Episode 2 - The Lost City of Gold and Combat discussion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=jQiLi6ev9zM

---

Daniel (@dungeondive) continues the solo RPG campaign with Eli and Cornelia, demonstrating hex crawl exploration, job completion, and the emergent storytelling that makes this system sing.

The posse heads into the mountains searching for the Lost City of Gold — a jobs-board quest with a 10-day timer. Along the way, a wilderness encounter creates a mysterious standing stone covered in undecipherable runes. When a wandering zombie leads them back to that same stone to lie beside another corpse, Daniel connects the dots: the stone attracts the restless dead, drawing wandering undead to their final rest. These narrative connections between random encounters are hex crawl's greatest strength.

Mountain yetis attack with rolling boulders (passed via agility checks), and the posse eventually discovers the golden city — finding 300 gold through treasure-hunting luck rolls before the ruins collapse.

The episode's second half tackles combat philosophy. Daniel explains his abstracted system: three ranges (ranged, melee with one hero, melee with both), no map tiles, tokens on character sheets. He demonstrates with Ancient Horrors and Bandits, discusses escape mechanics borrowed from the base game, and vents about void swarms whose defense-5 rating makes fights drag interminably. He's considering terrain elements from Assault on Doomrock's system to add tactical interest without reintroducing grid movement.
